title: Critical functions of L-selectin-mediated lymphocyte homing and recruitment
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"a9k36","text":"To analyze glycan function, enzyme digestion is one of the easiest method.  We successfully demonstrated the elimination of N-glycans, O-glycans and heparan sulfate on lymph node frozen section by N-glycanase (N-glycosidase F), O-sialoglycopeptidase, and heparitinases, respectively.   N-glycanase treatment usually needs denaturing condition such as SDS treatment, but this can be substituted with acetone treatment of the frozen section.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"p3nvi","text":"N-glycosidase F, Calbiochem.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1868","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"z3xmn","text":"O-sialoglycoprotein endopeptidase, Accurate Chemical and Scientific Corporation. \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gqvuf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gks0j","text":"Heparitinase I and II and heparinase, Seikagaku.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fix lymph node frozen sections with acetone for 15 min at room temperature.
Wash the sections with PBS 3 times and treated with the following enzymes.

Incubate the fixed lymph node frozen sections with 100 mU/ml N-glycosidase F in 10 mM HEPES-NaOH, pH 7.4, 0.1% Triton X-100, 0.1 M 2-mercaptoethanol and complete protease inhibitor (Roche) at 37°C for 2 h. Proceed to 6.
Incubate the sections with 0.1 ~ 1 mg/ml O-sialoglycoprotein endopeptidase 1 for in 10 mM HEPES-NaOH, pH 7.4 at 37°C for 2 h (ref. 1). Proceed to 6.
Incubate the sections with 25 mU/ml Heparitinase I and II and heparinase in PBS containing 1 mg/ml BSA, 1 mM CaCl 2 and the protease inhibitor at 37°C for 2 h. Proceed to 6.
The digested sections were washed with PBS containing 0.1 mg/ml BSA at least 3 times and subjected to further staining.

Stanadard immunostaining technique using antibodies specific for appropriate glycans can be done to evaluate the digestions. Lectin staining including L/E/P-selectin-IgM staining may be also applicable 2 .

When the sections are treated with N-glycanase, weak L/E-selectin staining might persist. In that case, fix the section with 4% paraformaldehyde at room temperature for 15 min, denature proteins with 2% SDS and 5% 2-mercaptoethanol at 65 °C for 10 min. For O-sialoglycopeptidase treatment, it is very difficult to obtain the best results and trial and error may be necessary.
The section should not be dried. Keep the sample in a moisture chamber

N-glycanse treatment removes all E/L-selectin-IgM binding in O-glycan deficient mice 2 . O-sialoglycopepeptidase digestion partially cleaves O-glycans but not always complete. Digestion with heparitinase I and II with heparinase eliminates all heparan sulfate as judged by staining with monoclonal antibody 10E4 (ref. 2).

Critical functions of N-glycans in L-selectin-mediated lymphocyte homing and recruitment by Junya Mitoma, Xingfeng Bao, Bronislawa Petryanik, Patrick Schaerli, Jean-Marc Gauguet, Shin-Yi Yu, Hiroto Kawashima, Hideo Saito, Kazuaki Ohtsubo, Jamey D Marth, Kay-Hooi Khoo, Ulrich H von Andrian, John B Lowe & Minoru Fukuda published in Nature Immunology on 03 Jul, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/ni1442
creator: Protocols Importer
